claim: ETH resolving above 2200 on the specified April 17 Binance noon-ET 1-minute close remains the clear base case, but the swarm’s mild below-market view survives synthesis: current Binance price context strongly supports Yes, yet a 94.5% market price still looks a bit too aggressive for a venue-specific single-minute crypto close with sub-24h tail risk still live.

frontmatter.contract_ambiguity_reason: small residual UI-versus-API and exact candle-label interpretation risk, but rules are explicit enough for practical use
independently_verified_points: Polymarket rules explicitly resolve on Binance ETH/USDT 12:00 ET 1m final close
Live Binance ETHUSDT remained around 2353 during synthesis-stage check
Binance 24h low was still above 2200 at 2308.5
Recent Binance 1m closes were clustered near 2353, not near threshold
Swarm consensus that main residual risk is exact-minute path risk was faithful to raw findings
frontmatter.verification_gap_summary: No near-settlement Apr 17 morning verification yet, so overnight shock risk remains only partially checked.
frontmatter.best_countercase_summary: A fast overnight or U.S.-morning selloff or Binance-specific wick into the exact noon ET minute could still flip this to No.
frontmatter.main_reason_for_disagreement: Remaining disagreement is mostly about how much probability to charge for exact-minute crypto tail risk versus treating the current cushion as nearly dispositive.
frontmatter.resolution_mechanics_summary: Yes resolves only if Binance ETH/USDT's 12:00 ET Apr 17 one-minute candle final close is strictly above 2200.
frontmatter.freshness_driver: ETH can still move materially before the Apr 17 12:00 ET resolving minute, so a morning Binance check would meaningfully update odds.
decision_blockers: No major contract blocker; main blocker is unverified near-settlement price path risk.
Residual minor ambiguity about Binance UI-versus-API representation is not zero.
Actionability is limited because final view remains below market only modestly.
frontmatter.next_checkpoint: Binance ETH/USDT check on Apr 17 morning, especially if price moves toward 2300 or below.

sections.Alpha summary: Market implied probability is 0.945. My post-synthesis range is 0.89 to 0.92 Yes. That leaves only a modest below-market edge, not a large one. The likely mispricing is that the market is treating a roughly 6-7% cushion as almost sufficient for near-certainty even though settlement depends on one exact Binance minute close.
sections.Input coverage: All five personas were available and usable: base-rate, catalyst-hunter, market-implied, risk-manager, and variant-view. No personas were missing. I critically checked the raw findings against the sidecars; the sidecars looked faithful and not materially distorted, though they were compressive. I relied mainly on raw persona findings plus a bounded synthesis-stage verification pass using Polymarket rules and live Binance data. Coverage is complete because all required lanes were present and broadly convergent.
sections.Market-implied baseline: The baseline to synthesize against is 94.5% Yes from the assignment and Polymarket page. The market ladder also looked internally coherent during verification, with 2200 around 95%, 2300 around low-70s, and 2400 around 30%, which argues against an obviously broken market.
sections.Syndicated probability estimate: Final syndicated estimate: 0.89 to 0.92 Yes. This is my own post-synthesis range, centered slightly above the swarm median of 0.90 only because independent checks confirmed the current cushion and low contract ambiguity, but still clearly below market due to exact-minute crypto path risk.
sections.Difference from swarm-implied center: This is not a material departure from the swarm-implied center of about 0.90. If anything I stayed close to it because the raw lanes were unusually aligned and the synthesis-stage verification confirmed the core factual setup rather than overturning it.
sections.Agreement or disagreement with market: I disagree modestly with the market. The market is directionally right that Yes is favored, but I do not think 94.5% is fully earned for a single-minute exchange-specific crypto print that is still almost a day away. This is more a confidence discount than a directional disagreement.
sections.Independent verification of edge: Independent verification quality is medium. I independently checked the Polymarket rule text and current Binance ETHUSDT data during synthesis. That confirmed the contract mechanics, current spot near 2353, a 24h low still above 2200, and recent 1m closes well above strike. What remains unverified is the actual Apr 17 morning path into settlement, which is precisely where the residual No risk lives. Because the core evidence is strong but still heavily dependent on the same venue that determines settlement, verification is medium rather than high.
sections.Compression toward market due to verific: No meaningful compression toward market was required beyond the swarm baseline. The synthesis-stage checks did not reveal stronger evidence that the market’s 94.5% confidence was right; they mainly confirmed the swarm’s narrower 0.88-0.91 view. I did not move materially toward market because the missing piece is near-settlement path verification, not evidence that tail risk is negligible.
sections.Timing and catalyst posture: The key checkpoint is the Apr 17 morning-to-noon ET window. The edge is more likely to compress toward market if ETH remains comfortably above 2300 into that window, and more likely to widen against market if ETH retraces toward the low 2200s or Binance-specific dislocation appears. Waiting for fresher data would improve accuracy, but for a tradeable short-horizon market it also reduces optionality and may erase any small edge.
sections.Decision blockers: There is no major contract ambiguity blocker. The real blocker is that the surviving disagreement is small and timing-sensitive: without a near-settlement refresh, the remaining edge versus market is modest and partly hostage to overnight volatility. Operator caution is warranted, but no major new research leg is strictly required.
sections.Implication for the question: The best current synthesis is still Yes, with high but not near-certain confidence. Operationally: above-2200 at the specific Binance noon ET close is more likely than not by a wide margin, but the market appears a bit too dismissive of exact-minute downside tail risk.
sections.Consensus across personas: All personas agreed Yes is the base case. All agreed the current Binance level in the low-to-mid 2300s leaves a meaningful cushion above 2200. All agreed the main residual risk is not broad ETH direction but narrow settlement-window path risk on one exact Binance 1-minute close. All agreed contract mechanics are fairly clear and that the market is at least slightly overconfident versus that mechanism.
sections.Key disagreements across personas: Disagreement was low-intensity and mostly weighting-based/market-pricing-based. Base-rate was most conservative at 0.88, putting more weight on realized crypto volatility and single-minute fragility. Catalyst-hunter was most bullish at 0.91, putting more weight on the absence of an identified scheduled bearish catalyst. The others clustered at 0.90. There was also a minor interpretive disagreement about how much to charge for UI/API or candle-label fragility, but no lane treated this as a major contract problem.
sections.Best countercase: The best countercase, represented most clearly by base-rate and risk-manager, is that a one-minute noon ET crypto settlement should retain more live tail risk than a 94.5% market price implies, especially if an overnight selloff, liquidation cascade, or Binance-specific wick lands exactly in the resolving candle.
sections.Encapsulated assumptions: Shared assumptions: Binance remains operationally normal; current spot materially above 2200 is informative for next-day noon probability; no major shock arrives before resolution. Contested assumptions: how much probability mass should still be assigned to a >6% downside move over the remaining window; how much practical risk remains from Binance UI/API or candle-interpretation mechanics. Fragile assumptions: that current calm price context persists into U.S. morning and that Binance settlement representation remains straightforward.
sections.Encapsulated evidence map: Strongest supporting evidence: Polymarket rules explicitly define the source of truth; live Binance ETHUSDT was around 2353 during synthesis; Binance 24h low was 2308.5, still above strike; recent minute closes remained clustered around 2353. Strongest contradictory evidence: crypto can move 6%+ in under a day; one exact minute determines resolution; venue-specific wick or operational issue would matter more than broader ETH references. Governing source-of-truth evidence is clear and favors a clean interpretation; ambiguous evidence is mostly about operational representation rather than the contract text itself.
sections.Evidence weighting: Most weight went to the Polymarket rules and direct Binance price context because settlement is venue-specific. I downweighted generic secondary ETH commentary because it is not the settlement source. I also downweighted any apparent comfort from broad ETH bullishness; the relevant issue is exact-minute threshold risk, not medium-term directional thesis.
sections.Counterpoints / strongest disconfirming: The strongest disconfirming evidence is not a current bearish signal but the structural reality that a single exact-minute crypto close can fail despite comfortable pre-settlement trading. The market may be undercharging the tail of a fast selloff or wick into the noon ET candle.
sections.Resolution or source-of-truth interpreta: The contract mechanics are practically usable and only mildly ambiguous. Yes requires Binance ETH/USDT, 1-minute candle, 12:00 ET on Apr 17, final close strictly above 2200. Other exchanges, other pairs, intraminute highs, or general daily closes do not matter. The only residual ambiguity is a small practical question around Binance website display versus API representation and exact candle labeling, but the raw persona work and synthesis check both suggest this is not large enough to dominate the forecast.
sections.Why this could create or destroy alpha: If the market is overpaying for apparent safety simply because spot is currently well above the strike, then a modest No edge exists. But this is exactly the kind of edge that can vanish if price stays firm into the final morning. Alpha here comes from correctly pricing narrow settlement mechanics and residual tail risk; it gets destroyed if that residual tail risk is already fully reflected or if new calm price action validates the market’s confidence.
sections.What would falsify this interpretation /: A fresh Apr 17 morning Binance check still showing ETH comfortably above 2300 with no unusual volatility would move me somewhat toward the market. A move toward the low 2200s, a sharp crypto risk-off headline, or Binance-specific pricing instability would move me materially lower. Evidence that the relevant Binance candle interpretation differs from current understanding would also change the view, though that currently looks unlikely.
sections.Highest-value next research: Single highest-value next check: direct Binance ETH/USDT level and volatility review on Apr 17 morning, as close to settlement as workflow permits.
sections.Source-quality assessment: Primary governing source class was first-party contract text plus first-party exchange data. The most important contextual source class was secondary public ETH price references, mainly useful as sanity checks. Evidence independence is medium because the best evidence all points back to the same underlying Binance market. Source-of-truth ambiguity is low-to-minor. The synthesis is not bottlenecked by missing personas, but it is bottlenecked by the unavoidable fact that the decisive minute is in the future.
sections.Verification impact: Yes, synthesis used additional verification beyond the persona findings. Cross-lane comparison confirmed the swarm was genuinely aligned rather than falsely convergent. Fresh synthesis-stage checks on Polymarket rules and Binance price data did not change direction, but they strengthened confidence in the factual setup and left the main unresolved issue exactly where the raw lanes said it was: near-settlement path risk. I did not find any major lane-level inconsistency; the sidecars were faithful to the raw findings.
sections.Persona contribution map: base-rate — strongest pushback against treating a 6-7% cushion as near-certainty; best outside-view argument for discounting market confidence. catalyst-hunter — best articulation that no clearly identified scheduled bearish catalyst was visible before noon ET, limiting reasons for a large immediate selloff. market-implied — best framing of market coherence, including strike-ladder sanity checks and why the market is broadly right on direction. risk-manager — best articulation of narrow-window path risk and timestamp-specific settlement fragility. variant-view — best formulation of the surviving minority-style caution: disagreement is about confidence degree, not direction.
sections.Reusable lesson signals: Possible durable lesson: in short-horizon crypto threshold markets, the real edge often comes from pricing exact-minute settlement mechanics rather than taking a broad directional stand. Possible underbuilt driver: venue-specific settlement-window microstructure or timestamp-resolution risk may deserve cleaner canonical treatment if these cases recur. Possible source-quality lesson: first-party exchange data plus rules text is the right core stack, but morning-of refresh matters disproportionately. Reusability confidence: medium.
sections.Orchestrator review suggestions: Review later for durable lesson: yes. Review later for driver candidate: yes. Review later for canon or linkage issue: yes. Review later for swarm-method issue: no. Reason: repeated exchange-specific minute-resolution markets likely justify cleaner canonical handling of settlement-window microstructure and Binance-global venue linkage.
sections.Recommended follow-up: Request a near-settlement refresh if operationally possible; otherwise treat this as a modest below-market Yes forecast with limited edge and no major unresolved contract blocker.
